paper_title: Simulations of the Holocene climate in Europe using an interactive downscaling within the iLOVECLIM model

methods.description: This method involves simulating the transient evolution of the climate during the last 11.5 kyr using the iLOVECLIM model. Two experiments were conducted: one with the standard model and another with downscaling applied to compute regional temperature and precipitation.

steps.description: Apply iLOVECLIM-1.0 and iLOVECLIM-1.1 to simulate the transient evolution of the climate during the last 11.5 kyr.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate climate evolution using iLOVECLIM model

steps.description: Perform two experiments: 11.5 K_Standard with the standard model on the T21 grid, and 11.5 K_Down with downscaling applied to the T21 grid.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct climate experiments with standard and downscaled models

steps.description: Force simulations with orbital forcings and atmospheric trace gas concentrations, varying annually for the 11.5-0 ka BP period.
steps.searchable_summary: apply orbital and trace gas forcings to simulations

steps.description: Prescribe constant ice sheet configurations and keep solar constant and aerosol levels fixed at pre-industrial levels.
steps.searchable_summary: set constant ice sheet and fixed solar/aerosol levels

steps.description: Present results of precipitation and temperature as anomalies relative to the pre-industrial period and compare downscaled results with standard version.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze and compare precipitation and temperature anomalies

methods.description: This section describes the components and capabilities of the iLOVECLIM model, which is used for climate simulations. It includes atmospheric, ocean, and vegetation models.

steps.description: Describe the iLOVECLIM model as a three-dimensional model with a simplified representation of the atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: describe iLOVECLIM model structure and components

steps.description: Explain the components of the model: ECBilt atmospheric model, CLIO sea ice ocean component, and VECODE terrestrial vegetation model.
steps.searchable_summary: explain ECBilt, CLIO, and VECODE components

steps.description: Discuss the model's application in simulating past and future climates, including the LGM, Holocene, and 21st century.
steps.searchable_summary: discuss iLOVECLIM applications in climate simulations
